reasoning:
The Nobel Peace Prize market has already resolved NO (since the 2025 prize recipients are announced in October and Trump did not win). Therefore, due to the logical IFF, this market will resolve YES if the second condition (a bilateral ceasefire in Ukraine before 2026) is also NO, and resolves NO if the second condition happens. Effectively, the question asks for the probability that there will NOT be a bilateral ceasefire in Ukraine before 2026. 

A formal bilateral ceasefire in the Russia-Ukraine war before 2026 remains unlikely. Even assuming US pressure on both sides to negotiate in 2025, reaching a documented, bilateral agreement to cease hostilities is extremely difficult given the firmly entrenched positions regarding territorial control. While temporary pauses or informal de-escalations might occur, a formal bilateral ceasefire before the end of 2025 has historically been priced around 20-35% in prediction markets. Thus, the probability of NO bilateral ceasefire (which leads to a YES resolution here) is likely around 65-75%.
